topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification blinked on my screen, a jarring intrusion into the quiet afternoon. My stomach clenched, a familiar, unpleasant tightening spreading through my chest. It was a request. From Mark. The name alone sent a cold wave washing over me, making the air in my room feel suddenly thin. I didn't even need to open it; I knew the content. A flicker of something unpleasant—a ghost from a painful past—stirred in my gut. I stared at the little profile picture, a distorted, sneering grin frozen in time, and my jaw tightened so hard I could feel the muscles ache. I swiftly swiped it away, a jerky, defensive movement. Why would he even bother? The thought was bitter, laced with a sharp, stinging resentment.

[story 2]

I picked up my phone, the glass cool against my palm, and the sight of the request made my breath hitch. A slow, deliberate wave of irritation washed over me, starting at my fingertips and creeping up my neck. It was him. The memory of his mocking laughter, the way his shadow used to loom over me in the hallway, flooded my mind, and a sour taste coated my mouth. I let the phone rest on my desk, my knuckles white where I gripped it. I didn't want to engage, but a strange, restless energy buzzed beneath my skin, a need to confront the unwelcome intrusion. I felt a tightening in my shoulders, a readiness to push back, even though my hands remained stubbornly still.

[story 3]

When the alert popped up, I froze mid-sentence, the casual conversation I was having dissolving into static. A deep, simmering displeasure began to bubble up inside me, hot and restless. It was him. That familiar shadow of malice flickered behind my eyes, and I could feel my jaw clench involuntarily. I looked at the request, and a profound sense of annoyance settled over me, heavy and suffocating. I wanted to slam the device down, to erase the connection entirely. My breathing became shallow, quickening slightly as I tried to control the tremor running through my hands. This was an unwelcome summons, and my entire body was preparing a silent resistance.
